meta.title: About Ingrid Ascanio — Miami Mortgage Broker
meta.description: From BankUnited underwriter to Pioneer Mortgage Funding branch manager: 20+ years on every side of the lending desk, now working for Miami borrowers.

hero.eyebrow: Ingrid Ascanio · Branch Manager & Loan Officer
hero.title: I know how the person reading your file thinks. I used to be her.
hero.body: Most loan officers learned lending from the sales side. I learned it from the inside — underwriting files, running processing teams, and defending homeowners in loss mitigation — before I ever originated a loan.
hero.body2: That order matters: it's why my files are structured to be approved, not just submitted.

timeline.title: Every side of the lending desk

items.name: Underwriter, BankUnited
items.desc: The person who says yes or no. I learned exactly what makes a file fundable — and the small, fixable things that sink applications that should have closed.

items.name: Processing Manager, Globex Lending
items.desc: Running the machinery between application and closing table. Speed, in this business, is operations — and I ran the operation.

items.name: Loss Mitigation Specialist, Legal Save
items.desc: Foreclosure defense and loan modifications — fighting for homeowners on the worst day of their financial lives. It permanently changed how seriously I take the phrase “the right loan.”

items.name: Branch Manager & Loan Officer, Pioneer Mortgage Funding
items.desc: All of it, working for you: banker's discipline, operator's speed, advocate's loyalty — with a broker's freedom to shop 50+ lenders instead of defending one bank's menu.

pull: “My goal is to think outside the box — within the guidelines — and structure the deal with a win for all.”

values.title: What you can hold me to

items.name: Structured, not submitted
items.desc: Anyone can forward your documents to a lender. Structuring means choosing the lender whose guidelines fit your file, presenting income the way that lender reads best, and answering underwriting's questions before they're asked.

items.name: The truth, early
items.desc: If your file needs six months of credit work before it's ready, you'll hear it in our first call — with a plan attached. False hope wastes your time; a real map doesn't.

items.name: In your language
items.desc: The whole process happens in English or en español — not just the small talk, but the documents, the explanations, and the closing. Miami deserves nothing less.

items.name: Reachable, humanly
items.desc: Offers get written on Sunday afternoons. Deadlines land on holidays. My clients have my cell, and 20 years in, I still answer it.

items.name: FAMP Miami — Vice President, 2025–2026
items.desc: Elected leadership in the Florida Association of Mortgage Professionals, Miami chapter.

items.name: FAMP Tradeshow Co-Chair, 2025–2026
items.desc: Co-chairing the chapter's flagship industry event.

cta.title: Twenty years of lending knowledge. Fifteen minutes of your time.
cta.body: Tell me where you are — dreaming, shopping, or under contract and nervous. I'll tell you exactly what happens next.
